page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 48 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance runs on the KVM hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions for data persistence. It also lacks dedicated GPU hardware, making it unsuitable for workloads requiring hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in the base configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.hfr7.12xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, aligning it with memory-optimized hardware classes. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from hyperthreading, though the lack of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff that shifts storage performance dependencies entirely to network-attached storage.

Given the hardware profile, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU execution. Suitable applications include in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, large-scale caching layers, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 server instance powered by a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8369HB processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, delivering 48 vCPUs alongside 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, categorizing it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, large caching layers, and enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density without hyperthreading overhead.
